title: Heap — Fix After Replace Root
description:
In a max-heap (1-indexed array), after replacing the root with the last element and decreasing size by 1, apply percolate_down to restore the heap property. Output the resulting heap.

Recall: always swap with the LARGEST child.
inputSpec: First line: n. Second line: n integers forming a valid max-heap EXCEPT possibly position 1 (the root has been replaced by t[n+1] and size is now n).
outputSpec: The restored max-heap: n integers, space-separated.

botCodes.code:
n=int(input())
a=[0]+list(map(int,input().split()))
k=1
while 2*k<=n:
    j=2*k
    if j+1<=n and a[j+1]>a[j]:j+=1
    if a[k]>=a[j]:break
    a[k],a[j]=a[j],a[k];k=j
print(*a[1:])

let n=ref 0
let a=Array.make 10002 0
let down k=let k=ref k in while 2* !k<= !n do let j=ref(2* !k) in if 2* !k+1<= !n&&a.(2* !k+1)>a.(2* !k) then j:=2* !k+1;if a.(!k)>=a.(!j) then k:= !n+1 else begin let t=a.(!k) in a.(!k)<-a.(!j);a.(!j)<-t;k:= !j end done
let()=n:=Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id;for i=1 to !n do a.(i)<-Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id done;down 1;for i=1 to !n do Printf.printf"%d%c" a.(i)(if i< !n then ' ' else '\n') done
